titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Matthew Nathan to Alexander McDowell, 3 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Matthew Nathan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir Matthew Nathan to Alexander McDowell. Nathan is writing to McDowell in relation to unemployment in the mills and factories in Belfast. The letter thanks Mr McDowell for his assurance that rumours of possible unemployment in the weaving mills are unfounded.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising. Alexander McDowell, Esq., was the Joint Director of Ireland for the Ministry of Munitions.

correspAction.placeName: Coate's Buildings, Castle Street, Belfast, County Antrim, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
3rd November 1915. Dear Mr. McDowell, I am much obliged to you for your letter
of yesterday's date telling me that there is no
foundation for the rumour of probable unemployment
of women owing to shortage of material for
the spinning mills. I was very glad to hear
this not only on account of the women but also
on account of the important industry employing
them. Your truly, Alexander McDowell, Esq., Ministry of Munitions, Coate's Buildings, Castle Street, Belfast.
